window: Phase 6 A.5 — executed same-day as P0.1 per ahead-of-schedule directive
notes: Gemma 4 had to be substituted: super-plan said `gemma4:27b` but Ollama registry lists `gemma4:26b` (26B MoE with 3.8B active) and `gemma4:31b` (31B dense). Both were pulled; 26B lives on Mac Mini, 31B on MacBook Pro via LM Studio MLX 4-bit (identifier `gemma4-31b`).

gemma4:26b on Mac Mini M4 Pro 24GB was benched in a prior pass and exceeded the 120s per-sample timeout on every request. Cold-prompt inference ≈100s; long prompts >120s. Per plan §A.6 veto (≥5% regression = KEEP), gemma4:26b → NOT a viable challenger for Mac Mini routing.

financial_analysis incumbent run hit LM Studio RAM exhaustion: with gemma4-31b holding 28GB, qwen3-14b's 8GB JIT-load failed. Benchmark captured partial data (inbox_triage only); financial_analysis + code_review runs require sequential model load/unload, deferred.

financial_analysis._partial: Incumbent qwen3-14b: 0/5 samples succeeded (LM Studio RAM guardrail blocked JIT-load while gemma4-31b held 28GB). Re-run with sequential model loading needed.

code_review._not_run: Skipped after financial_analysis RAM failure. Per plan §7.1, further challenger tests are optional after inbox_triage veto fires.

veto_gate_decisions.inbox_triage: KEEP INCUMBENT — gemma4-31b is 15% below phi4-mini-reasoning quality; veto gate (≥5% regression) fires cleanly.
veto_gate_decisions.financial_analysis: DEFERRED — incumbent incomplete due to infra, not model quality.
